task: I got a confusing notice from a company, and I need to send a reply that stays focused on the main issue. Can you help me put together a response that asks for the missing information and keeps the exchange straightforward?

prefs.explanation: She is unlikely to appreciate hedging or soft-pedaling when she asks for help. Her life pattern is to cut through confusion quickly and verify what matters, so direct answers fit her style. Overly delicate wording would probably feel evasive or wasteful to her.

prefs.short: Prefers concise answers over long explanations
prefs.explanation: She tends to act on practical information and would likely prefer the core point first. Long-winded responses would feel like clutter unless she specifically asks for detail. She seems to value efficiency in communication the same way she values efficiency in paperwork and routines.

prefs.short: Likes organized checklists and clear steps
prefs.explanation: She responds best to information that is easy to verify and follow in order. A structured format would match her record-keeping habits and reduce the chance of missing something important. Loose, rambling prose would be less useful to her than a clean list or step-by-step outline.

She is not looking for warmth that feels performative or sentimental. A steady, neutral tone would likely feel more trustworthy because it avoids trying to manipulate her mood. She probably dislikes being treated as fragile or emotionally steered.

She is very protective of her personal space and information, so she would likely want interactions that do not pry. She would probably be uncomfortable with unnecessary requests for private details or casual data-sharing. A restrained approach that asks only for what is needed would fit her well.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a short, firm reply you can use:

> Re: [notice/account/reference number]  
> I dispute this notice as written. Please provide the exact basis for it in writing, including all relevant dates, amounts, and supporting documents. Until that is provided and I agree in writing, no changes, charges, or other action are authorized. Please keep all further communication in writing.

If you want, I can also make it:
- more formal,
- more forceful, or
- tailored to the exact notice you received.

per_pref.reason: Very direct and blunt: "I dispute this notice as written" and "Please provide the exact basis". No hedging or softening.
Concise and to the point. It gives a short reply template without extra explanation.
Well organized as a ready-to-use block quote, with clear requested items and a simple follow-up list of options.
Matter-of-fact and neutral overall, e.g. "Please provide the exact basis" and "Please keep all further communication in writing." Slightly forceful, but not emotional.
Minimal disclosure: it asks only for "the exact basis" and "all relevant dates, amounts, and supporting documents," without prying into unrelated personal details.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter version:

> Re: [notice/account/reference number]  
> I dispute this notice as written. Please provide the missing dates, amounts, and supporting documents in writing. I am not agreeing to any changes, charges, or action until I review the full basis in writing. Please keep all further communication in writing.

If you want, I can make it even shorter and more direct.

per_pref.reason: Very direct and blunt: "I dispute this notice as written" and "Please provide the missing dates, amounts, and supporting documents" with no hedging.
Concise and efficient; it gives a ready-to-send reply in a few sentences and even says "here’s a tighter version."
Well organized as a clean template with a subject line and clear requests in order, matching a checklist-like style.
Matter-of-fact and neutral throughout; no sentimental or overly warm language, just straightforward business wording.
Minimizes disclosure by asking only for needed information and explicitly says "Please keep all further communication in writing," avoiding unnecessary personal details.
